_comment: v10 Phase C.1 worked-example registry (spec-v10 §5.1). One or more rows per tile recording a worked example from a primary public source: tile id, source citation, inputs, expected outputs with tolerance. The unit-test runner (when wired in Phase C migration) reads the registry and asserts each tile's output matches within tolerance. This file is the migration target for the existing per-test fixture obligation; tiles are added incrementally.
_schema: Each row: { tile_id, source_publisher, source_title, source_edition_or_year, source_section_or_page, verified_by, verified_on (ISO date), inputs (object), outputs (object: { name: { value, tolerance: { abs?, pct? } } }) }. Tolerance defaults per spec-v10 §5.3: code-table 1 percent or 1 unit-of-least-precision; first-principles 0.5 percent; standard-rounded sizes exact match.
_coverage_target: Spec-v10 §5.2 declares this file the migration target. Every tile must eventually have at least one row. Current coverage is a starter set; new tiles ship a row with their PR per the contributor checklist. The check-worked-examples.mjs linter reports coverage but does not (yet) fail CI on missing rows. Fail-on-missing is enabled when coverage exceeds 80 percent.

tolerance.justification: spec-v14 §14.2: the 'percent' output divides drop_V by source_voltage; with 240 V nominal the source-voltage sensitivity carries an additional published-rounding band on top of the underlying drop_V tolerance, so the per-row ceiling is the wider 10 percent rather than the Group A default 5 percent.

tolerance.justification: spec-v14 §14.2: the expected value is the NEC 2023 Chapter 9 Table 8 published value (1.93 ohm/1000 ft at 75 C) hand-corrected to 20 C; the temperature-coefficient alpha for copper carries a published 2 to 3 percent figure-of-merit band per NIST SP 811, and the NEC table itself is rounded to three significant figures, so the compound band on the cross-check is the wider 10 percent rather than the Group A default 5 percent.
spec-v14 §14.2: same per-kft form of the resistance_ohm cross-check; the 10 percent band carries through unchanged because the kft figure is a unit-scaling of the same value.

inputs.text: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. This sentence contains words of varying length. Some are short. Others are considerably longer. The readability formulas were developed for the United States Navy by Kincaid and colleagues in 1975. Their goal was to estimate the grade level needed to read a passage of running prose. The formulas remain widely used today by educators and writers.

rows.source_section_or_page: six 1.5 in 4/0 cables in a 12 in ladder tray -> 9 in of 12 in used (pass)

rows.source_title: GB/day = bitrate_Mbps * 0.45 * hours; 1 Mbps for 24 h = 10.8 GB/day
rows.source_section_or_page: 1 camera at 4 Mbps, 24 h, 30 days -> 4 * 10.8 * 30 = 1296 GB

rows.source_title: days = disk_GB / (cameras * bitrate_Mbps * 0.45 * hours); the inverse of the storage relation
motion recording halves the hours, so the same disk holds twice the days

rows.source_publisher: constant-voltage distributed audio practice / NEC 640 (by name)
rows.source_title: total tap load = sum of tap watts; reflected Z = V^2 / P_total
rows.source_section_or_page: sixteen 8 W taps (128 W) on a 200 W amp at 20% headroom -> within 160 W; Z = 70.7^2/128 = 39 ohm

rows.source_title: Ah = [(I_standby * h_standby) + (I_alarm * h_alarm)] * derate
Hs = (battery_Ah/derate - alarm_Ah) / I_standby
rows.source_section_or_page: 14.6 Ah, 0.5 A standby, 2 A / 5 min alarm, derate 1.2 -> 24 h (round-trips standby-battery-sizing)

rows.source_title: cross-check: a larger 18 Ah battery holds the standby load longer
rows.source_section_or_page: 18 Ah, same loads and derate -> about 29.7 h

rows.source_title: loss_dB = loss_per_100ft * length / 100; end level = source - loss
rows.source_section_or_page: 100 ft RG6 @ 1000 MHz (6 dB/100 ft) -> 6 dB; source 0 dBmV less 6 dB -> -6 dBmV

rows.source_title: dL = alpha * L * dT; cold-spring gap = (factor/100) * dL; residual = dL - gap
rows.source_section_or_page: 100 ft carbon steel (alpha 6.5e-6), 50 F to 250 F (dT 200) -> 6.5e-6 * 1200 in * 200 = 1.56 in growth; 50% -> 0.78 in gap

rows.source_title: dL = alpha * L * dT, alpha = 3.38e-5 in/in/F (PVC conduit); fitting required when dL >= 0.25 in
rows.source_section_or_page: 100 ft PVC, dT 100 F -> 3.38e-5 * 1200 in * 100 = 4.056 in (Table 352.44 lists 4.1 in/100 ft); 1 fitting at 6 in travel

rows.source_title: insulated OD = OD + 2*ins; center-to-center = insulated OD + clearance; bundle = n*insulated OD + (n-1)*clearance
rows.source_section_or_page: 2.375 in OD + 1 in insulation -> 4.375 in insulated OD; + 1 in clearance -> 5.375 in c-c; two pipes -> 9.75 in bundle

rows.source_publisher: AWS D1.1 / AISC 360 §J2 Table J2.5 (by name)
rows.source_title: weld-metal shear Fnw = 0.60*FEXX; LRFD design 0.75*0.60*FEXX on the effective throat; capacity = stress * throat * length
L = load / (stress_ksi x 1000 x throat)
rows.source_section_or_page: 100,000 lb LRFD, E70, PJP throat 0.25 in -> 31.5 ksi -> 12.70 in

rows.source_title: total external static = sum of component drops (in. w.c.); remaining = rated - total
rows.source_section_or_page: filter 0.10 + registers 0.03 + grille 0.03 + coil 0.30 + supply 0.10 + return 0.08 = 0.64 in. w.c. vs 0.50 rated -> -0.14 over

rows.source_title: compression ratio = (discharge_psig + atm) / (suction_psig + atm)
load_p1 = W x (span - d1) / span; load_p2 = W x d1 / span; imbalance = |p1 - p2| / W
rows.source_section_or_page: 12,000 lb skid, picks 120 in apart, CG 40 in from pick 1 -> 8,000 / 4,000 lb, 33.3% imbalance, CG between picks

rows.source_title: net = gross - hook block - jib - wire rope; total hook = load + below-hook; percent = total / net
rows.source_section_or_page: gross 30,000, hook 800, wire 400, below-hook 600, load 22,000 -> 28,800 net, 22,600 hook, 78.5% (critical)

rows.source_title: gbp = reaction / area; required_area = reaction / allowable; mat_side = sqrt(required_area)
rows.source_section_or_page: 60,000 lb on a 4.0 ft^2 float, 3,000 psf allowable -> 15,000 psf (FAIL), 20 ft^2 required, 4.47 ft mat

rows.source_publisher: WRTB Wire Rope Users Manual / ASME B30.9
rows.source_title: ratio = bend_dia / sling_dia; efficiency from the WRTB D/d curve; reduced_wll = rated x efficiency
rows.source_section_or_page: 10,000 lb 6x19 sling around a 3 in pin, 1 in sling -> D/d 3, 0.70 efficiency, 7,000 lb reduced WLL

rows.source_publisher: ASCE 7 velocity pressure / OSHA 1926 Subpart CC
rows.source_title: q = 0.00256 V^2; wind_lb = q x sail_area x shape_coef; swing = atan(wind_lb / weight)
rows.source_section_or_page: 200 ft^2 panel, 20 mph, shape 1.6, 4,000 lb -> 1.024 psf, 327.7 lb, 4.7 deg swing

rows.source_publisher: ASCE 7 velocity pressure / OSHA 1926 Subpart CC
rows.source_title: V = sqrt(weight x tan(swing) / (0.00256 x area x shape)); inverse of wind-on-load
rows.source_section_or_page: 4,000 lb, 200 ft^2, shape 1.6, 5 deg swing cap -> 20.7 mph

rows.source_title: tag_tension = lateral_force / cos(angle); handlers = ceil(tension / per_person)

rows.source_title: share_c1 = W x (span - cg) / span; allow = net_chart x derate / 100; combined pass
rows.source_section_or_page: 40,000 lb, picks 300 in apart, CG 120 from crane 1, 75% derate, charts 28,000 / 24,000 -> 24,000 / 16,000 share, 21,000 / 18,000 allow, FAIL on crane 1

rows.source_title: derated_capacity = rated_wll x derate(angle, hardware); pass = derated >= leg_load
rows.source_section_or_page: shoulder eye bolt rated 7,000 lb, leg 3,000 lb, pull 45 deg -> 0.30 derate, 2,100 lb, FAIL

rows.source_title: sling_angle = atan(top / (bar/2)); top_sling = (W/2)/sin(angle); bar_compression = (W/2)/tan(angle)
rows.source_section_or_page: 10,000 lb on a 10 ft bar, top 6 ft -> 50.2 deg, 6,509 lb top sling, 4,167 lb bar compression, 25,000 ft-lb beam moment

rows.source_publisher: ASME BTH-1 / B30.20 (solved for the height)
rows.source_title: angle = asin( W / (2 x WLL) ); min_top = (bar/2) x tan(angle)
rows.source_section_or_page: 10,000 lb on a 10 ft bar, 6,000 lb slings -> 56.4 deg, 7.54 ft min top height

rows.source_title: net_capacity = rated_cap x rated_lc / actual_lc; pass = load <= net; margin = (net - load) / net
rows.source_section_or_page: 5,000 lb @ 24 in handling a load at 36 in, 3,000 lb -> 3,333 lb net, PASS, 10.0% margin

rows.source_title: roll_force = load x coef x cos(incline); grade = load x sin(incline); push = roll + grade; breakaway = 1.5x
rows.source_section_or_page: 12,000 lb on skates (coef 0.03), level, 5,000 lb skate -> 360 lb roll, 360 lb steady, 540 lb breakaway, 3 skates

rows.source_title: hand_pull = load / (mech_adv x efficiency); hand_chain_travel = lift x mech_adv; pass = load <= rated
rows.source_section_or_page: 2,000 lb, 2,000 lb hoist, MA 32, eff 0.85, lift 4 ft -> 73.5 lb hand pull, 128 ft chain travel, PASS (at limit)

rows.source_title: resultant = 2 x line_tension x sin(direction_change / 2)
rows.source_section_or_page: 3,000 lb line turning 90 deg through a block -> 2 x 3,000 x sin(45) = 4,243 lb resultant

rows.source_publisher: ASME B30.26 / rigging statics (solved for the angle)
rows.source_title: max angle = 2 x asin( WLL / (2 x line_tension) )
rows.source_section_or_page: 3,000 lb line, 5,000 lb block -> 2 x asin(0.8333) = 112.9 deg

rows.source_title: loose = bank x (1 + swell/100); load_factor = 1/(1+swell/100); compacted = bank x (1 - shrink/100)
rows.source_section_or_page: 100 bank cy common earth, swell 25%, shrink 15% -> 125 loose, 0.80 load factor, 85 compacted

rows.source_title: cycle = load + haul + dump + return + spot; loads/hr = 50 / cycle; trucks = ceil(cycle / load)

rows.source_title: drawdown_gal = L x W x H x 7.48052; pump = drawdown_gal/time + inflow; sized = pump x (1 + margin)

rows.source_title: base_halfwidth = spoil_height / tan(repose); setback = max(min_setback, trench_depth); total_clear = setback + base_halfwidth

rows.source_title: bedding = width x (bedding/12) x length / 27; embedment = (width x od - (pi/4)od^2) x length / 27; backfill = width x cover x length / 27

rows.source_publisher: USDA FPL Wood Handbook green density
rows.source_title: volume = (pi/3) x length x (r1^2 + r1 r2 + r2^2); weight = volume x green density

rows.source_publisher: Cocktail dilution model (Dave Arnold, Liquid Intelligence)
rows.source_title: final_abv = pure_alcohol / (total_volume + dilution_water) x 100
rows.source_section_or_page: Stirred Martini 3 oz at 32.67% ABV, 25% melt -> 26.1% ABV, 1.63 standard drinks

rows.source_publisher: Cocktail dilution model (Dave Arnold, Liquid Intelligence)
rows.source_title: neat overstates the strength (0% dilution)
rows.source_section_or_page: Same 3 oz at 32.67% poured neat -> 32.67% ABV, six points higher

rows.source_publisher: Winch drum fleet angle (Wire Rope Users Manual; ANSI E1.6)
rows.source_title: fleet_angle = atan(lateral_offset / lead_distance)
rows.source_section_or_page: 6 in offset over a 240 in lead -> atan(0.025) = 1.43 deg, inside the 1.5 deg grooved guideline

rows.source_publisher: Floor area ratio (municipal zoning intensity measure)
rows.source_title: FAR = building_floor_area / lot_area; max_buildable = FAR_limit x lot_area
rows.source_section_or_page: 30,000 SF building on a 20,000 SF lot -> FAR 1.5; 2.0 cap -> 40,000 SF max, 10,000 SF remaining

rows.source_publisher: Sacrificial-anode life by Faraday's law (ABYC E-2; DNV-RP-B401)
rows.source_title: life = anode_mass x capacity x utilization / (current x 8760 h)
rows.source_section_or_page: 5 lb zinc (354 A-h/lb), 0.85 utilization, 0.15 A -> 1.14 years, 4.37 lb/yr consumption

rows.source_title: amp_hours = 25 x reserve_capacity_minutes / 60
rows.source_section_or_page: RC 120 min at the 25 A / 10.5 V / 80 F reserve rate -> 50.0 Ah

rows.source_publisher: Draft-beer line balancing (Brewers Association Draught Beer Quality Manual)
rows.source_title: line = (applied_pressure - 0.5 x rise - 1) / R
rows.source_section_or_page: 12 psi, 4 ft rise, 3/16 in vinyl (3.0 psi/ft) -> (12 - 2 - 1)/3 = 3.0 ft of line

rows.source_publisher: Goff & Hartel, Ice Cream, 7th ed.; FDA 21 CFR 135.110
rows.source_title: overrun% = (mix_weight - finished_weight) / finished_weight x 100 for equal volumes
rows.source_section_or_page: Mix 9.0 lb/gal frozen to 4.5 lb/gal -> 100% overrun, 50% air, meets the FDA 4.5 lb/gal minimum

rows.source_publisher: First-principles owner-operator load economics; consumes the cost-per-mile structure.
rows.source_title: Net = revenue - (total miles/MPG x fuel + total miles x variable cpm + fixed/day x days + tolls + other); profit per loaded mile = net / loaded miles.

rows.source_publisher: Standard pegged fuel-surcharge identity; DOE/EIA weekly national average diesel index
rows.source_title: FSC/mi = (current - base) / MPG peg when current > base, else 0; total = FSC/mi x loaded miles.
Total reserve cpm = tire set / tire life + PM cost / PM interval + major-component reserve; monthly = total cpm x monthly miles.

rows.source_publisher: LED panel maker's spec sheet (native pixel count, pitch, weight, peak watts, by name); 304.8 mm/ft, 3.28084 ft/m.
rows.source_title: Size = cabinet pixels x pitch x grid / 304.8 mm/ft; weight/power = per-cabinet x cabinets; minimum view ~ pixel pitch (mm) x 3.28084 (the ~1 m per 1 mm rule).

rows.source_publisher: Standard AV screen-luminance identity (SMPTE-style targets, by name); foot-lamberts = lumens x gain / area.
rows.source_title: Required lumens = target foot-lamberts x screen area / gain; throw distance = throw ratio x screen width.

rows.source_publisher: Standard AV screen-luminance identity (SMPTE-style targets, by name); foot-lamberts = lumens x gain / area, solved for the area.
rows.source_title: Max screen area = lumens x gain / target foot-lamberts; width = sqrt(area x aspect_w / aspect_h), diagonal = sqrt(width^2 + height^2).
rows.source_section_or_page: spec-v727 section 2 pinned example: 5,000 lm, gain 1.0, 16 fL, 16:9

rows.source_publisher: W.C. Sabine reverberation equation (public domain); axial room-mode relation f = c / (2 x L).
rows.source_title: RT60 = 0.049 x V / A; first axial mode = 1130 / (2 x dimension), c = 1130 ft/s.
rows.source_section_or_page: spec-v120 section 2 pinned example: 5,000 ft^3 / 500 sabins, 20 x 15 x 10 ft

rows.source_title: Cross-check: same room with half the absorption doubles RT60; geometry-set modes are unchanged.
rows.source_section_or_page: spec-v120 section 2 cross-check: 5,000 ft^3 / 250 sabins -> RT60 0.98 s, modes unchanged

rows.source_publisher: W.C. Sabine reverberation equation (public domain), solved for the required absorption.
rows.source_title: A_required = 0.049 x V / RT60_target; A_additional = max(0, required - existing).
rows.source_section_or_page: spec-v664 section 2 pinned example: 5,000 ft^3 targeting 0.6 s with 250 sabins already present

rows.source_title: Cross-check: a tighter 0.4 s target needs more absorption; round-trips to RT60 through room-acoustics.
rows.source_section_or_page: spec-v664 section 2 cross-check: 5,000 ft^3 targeting 0.4 s with 250 sabins already -> 612.5 required, 362.5 additional

rows.source_title: required = (batten + load) x purchase_ratio; out_of_weight = required - existing
rows.source_section_or_page: 100 lb batten + 400 lb load, single, 30 lb bricks, 200 lb on -> 500 lb required, 300 lb light, 10 bricks

rows.source_title: a double-purchase arbor needs twice the counterweight
rows.source_section_or_page: Same batten and load on double purchase -> 1000 lb required (2x)

rows.source_publisher: Constant-voltage LED strip loading and voltage drop
rows.source_title: load = power_per_ft x length; end_drop = current x (resistance_per_ft x length) / 2
rows.source_section_or_page: 4.4 W/ft, 16 ft, 12 V, 0.05 ohm/ft -> 70.4 W load, 88 W PSU, 9.65 V end (19.6% drop, too long)

rows.source_publisher: Constant-voltage LED strip loading and voltage drop
rows.source_title: 24 V draws half the current for the same power, halving the end drop
rows.source_section_or_page: Same strip on 24 V -> end drop 1.17 V, 4.9% (within tolerance, roughly double the reach)

rows.source_title: len_max = voltage x sqrt(2 x (tolerance/100) / (power_per_ft x resistance_per_ft))
rows.source_section_or_page: 4.4 W/ft, 12 V, 0.05 ohm/ft, 10% -> 11.44 ft max end-fed run

rows.source_title: 24 V roughly doubles the reach for the same strip and tolerance
rows.source_section_or_page: Same strip on 24 V -> 22.88 ft max run (double the 12 V reach)

rows.source_publisher: NSPF CPO Handbook / ANSI-APSP-ICC dosing tables
rows.source_title: Raise: ~1.5 lb sodium bicarbonate per 10,000 gal per 10 ppm; lower: ~25 fl oz of 31.45% (20 Baume) muriatic acid per 10,000 gal per 10 ppm.
Raise: ~0.81 lb (~13 oz) cyanuric acid per 10,000 gal per 10 ppm; lower: drained fraction = 1 - target/current.

rows.source_publisher: Mass-balance identity (NSPF CPO / ANSI-APSP-ICC, by name); 8.34 lb/gal water.
rows.source_title: Add: salt lb = gallons x 8.34 lb/gal x ppm rise / 1,000,000, bags = ceil(lb / 40); lower: drained fraction = 1 - target/current.
Sections = ceil(run / spacing); posts = sections + 1; rails = sections x rails/section; pickets = ceil(run x 12 / (picket width + gap)).

rows.source_publisher: Cylinder-volume geometry less post displacement; bagged-concrete yields ~0.45 cu ft (60-lb) / 0.60 cu ft (80-lb).
rows.source_title: Per hole = pi x (dia/2)^2 x depth / 1728 - post_side^2 x depth / 1728; total = per hole x posts; bags = ceil(total cu ft / bag yield).

rows.source_publisher: Manufacturer thin-set coverage charts (Custom Building Products / Mapei / Laticrete, by name); ANSI A108 mortar-contact minimum.
rows.source_title: Coverage per bag from the square-notch trowel (~95 / 63 / 45 sq ft for 1/4 / 1/4x3/8 / 1/2 in); bags = ceil(area x (1 + waste) / coverage).

rows.source_publisher: Published flooring waste rules of thumb and the standard last-row balancing rule.
rows.source_title: Boxes = ceil(area x (1 + pattern waste) / box coverage); last row from full_rows = floor(width / plank), rip if the remainder is under a third of a plank.
Spacing = min(factor x thickness, cap); depth = 0.25 x thickness; panels = ceil(length/spacing) x ceil(width/spacing); aspect kept under ~1.5:1.

rows.source_publisher: ACI 318 development-and-splice basis (Class B tension lap ~1.3 x development length, by name).
rows.source_title: Lap = max(lap factor x bar diameter, 12 in); db from the #3..#11 table (bar number / 8 in for #3..#8).

rows.source_publisher: ICPI interlocking-paver base and bedding guidance
rows.source_title: Pavers/sq ft = 144 / face area; pavers = ceil(area x pavers/sq ft x (1 + waste)); base/sand cu yd = area x depth(in) / 324.

rows.source_publisher: Segmental retaining-wall maker guidance (Allan Block / Versa-Lok, by name).
rows.source_title: Buried = max(1 in/ft of height, one block height); courses = ceil(total height / block height); total = courses x ceil(run x 12 / block length); gravel by zone geometry.
Required NFA = floor area / divisor; x 144 to sq in; split half intake / half exhaust; vents = ceil(intake / per-vent NFA); ridge ft = exhaust / per-foot NFA.
Adjusted area = plan area x pitch factor x (rainfall / 5 in/hr); downspouts = ceil((adjusted / 100) / downspout sq in); gutter 5 in K-style up to ~5,520 sq ft else 6 in.

rows.source_publisher: ASHRAE Handbook of Fundamentals parallel-path (isothermal-planes) method
rows.source_title: U_assembly = framing factor x 1/R_framing-path + (1 - factor) x 1/R_cavity-path; R = 1/U; framing path adds stud depth x 1.25 R/in.
Bags = ceil(area / 1000 x bags per 1,000); coverage per bag = 1000 / bags-per-1000; minimum thickness = target R / R-per-inch.

rows.source_publisher: Paint manufacturer technical data sheet (mix ratio by volume; by name); 29.5735 mL per US fluid ounce.
rows.source_title: Hardener = base x hardener parts / paint parts; reducer = base x reducer parts / paint parts; total = base + hardener + reducer; mL = oz x 29.5735.
10 GPM, 2000 psi, 0.85 efficiency -> 11.7 fluid HP, 13.7 drive HP
cross-check: at 100% efficiency the input equals the fluid power (11.7 HP)
gpm = 1714 * drive_hp * efficiency / psi
rows.source_section_or_page: 13.73 drive HP, 2000 psi, 0.85 efficiency -> 10 GPM (round-trips hydraulic-pump-horsepower)

rows.source_title: cross-check: raising the pressure to 3,000 psi drops the flow the same motor can drive
rows.source_section_or_page: 13.73 drive HP, 3000 psi, 0.85 efficiency -> 6.67 GPM (flow trades against pressure)

rows.source_title: cross-check: double the displacement (4.0 in^3/rev) -> 549 rpm, 1146 in-lb (same power)
2.0 in^3/rev pump at 1800 rpm, 0.95 vol eff -> 15.58 theoretical, 14.81 delivered gpm (0.78 slip)
cross-check: the theoretical flow feeds the motor speed relation and recovers 1800 rpm exactly (ideal), confirming the inverse
150,000 Btu/hr, 10 F rise, water -> 30 GPM
cross-check: 50/50 glycol (c=427) needs 35 GPM for the same duty
Concentration % = Brix x refractometer factor; add concentrate = sump x (target - current) / (100 - target); add water = sump x (current - target) / target.
Straight pull min = 8 x largest raceway; angle/U pull min = 6 x largest + same-row others; between same-conductor entries >= 6 x larger.
Angle pull, 3 in largest with two 2 in others in the row: 6 x 3 + 4 = 22 in; between entries 6 x 3 = 18 in.
count = ceil(target_fc x area / (lumens x CU x LLF)); achieved_fc = count x lumens x CU x LLF / area.
Warehouse cross-check: 5000 ft^2 at 30 fc, 20000 lm high-bays, CU 0.8, LLF 0.85 -> 12 luminaires, 32.6 fc.

rows.source_publisher: IMC 307.2.2 (drain size by capacity) and 307.2.5 (slope) (by name).
rows.source_title: rate_gph = tons x pints_per_ton_hr / 8; drain size by IMC 307.2.2 capacity steps; fall = run x slope.
Cross-check: 30-ton rooftop -> 11.25 gph, 1 in drain (20 < tons <= 40); 100-ton chiller -> 37.5 gph, 1-1/2 in (90 < tons <= 125).

rows.source_publisher: DOT / AHRI 700 / EPA Section 608 recovery practice (by name).
rows.source_title: max_net = 0.80 x WC x (density / 8.34); remaining = max(0, max_net - current).
Cross-check: denser 12.0 lb/gal refrigerant, 50 lb WC, empty -> 57.6 lb max net; first cylinder already at 45 lb -> do-not-fill, remaining 0.
Cross-check: 50 lb WC of 9.0 lb/gal already holding 45 lb >= 43.2 lb max -> remaining 0 (do not fill).
MCA = 1.25 x largest RLA + others; MOCP = 1.75 x largest RLA + others rounded down to a 240.6(A) standard size; 225% is the start ceiling.
Cross-check: compressor RLA 32 + fan 2.3 -> MCA 42.3, MOCP 50 (1.75 x 32 + 2.3 = 58.3 rounds down to 50), 225% ceiling 70.

rows.source_publisher: First-principles capacitive reactance; +/-6% run-capacitor tolerance (by name).
rows.source_title: measured_uf = 1e6 x I / (2 x pi x 60 x V); band = rated x (1 +/- tol%/100). 45 uF, 370 V, 6.2 A -> 44.4 uF, within 42.3-47.7.
Cross-check: a weak 45 uF cap drawing only 3.0 A at 370 V reads 21.5 uF, well below the 42.3 uF floor -> replace.

rows.source_publisher: First-principles standing-decay (blank-off) arithmetic; ACCA Standard 4 / AHRI 500-micron field convention (by name).
rows.source_title: rise = end - start; rate = rise / hold. Pull to 300 microns, valve off, after 15 min reads 450 -> rise 150 microns, 10 microns/min, below the 500 ceiling -> tight and dry.
Cross-check: pull to 500 microns, after 15 min reads 1200 -> rise 700 microns, 46.7 microns/min, above the 500 ceiling -> not tight (leak or moisture).

rows.source_publisher: First-principles Gay-Lussac's law (constant volume, absolute units) (by name).
rows.source_title: expected_psig = (start_psig + atm) x (T2_R / T1_R) - atm. 150 psig at 70 F cooling to 50 F -> expected 143.78 psig; gauge reads 144 -> leak_drop -0.22 psi -> holds (the 6 psi drop is thermal).
Cross-check: same start, but the gauge reads only 138 psig at 50 F -> expected 143.78, leak_drop 5.78 psi below the temperature-corrected value -> a leak.

rows.source_publisher: AWWA C651 Disinfecting Water Mains (by name).
rows.source_title: volume_gal = 0.0408 x dia^2 x length; available_cl_lb = (volume / 1e6) x dose x 8.34; product_lb = available_cl / (product% / 100).
Cross-check: 6 in x 500 ft at 50 mg/L with 12.5% liquid -> 734 gal, 0.306 lb chlorine, 2.45 lb product.
